mnemonics: Hadoop Awakens Data In Clustered Havens
keyPoints: Hadoop is an open-source, distributed computing framework written in Java.
It was created to process large data sets and is particularly useful for big data analytics.
RDBMS (Relational Database Management System) is not suitable for handling large volumes of data due to its scalability issues.
Hadoop is a good alternative to RDBMS for big data processing as it can handle large datasets in parallel.
Hadoop has a rich history, starting in 2005 as a side project at Yahoo! Research.
In 2006, the Apache Software Foundation took over Hadoop and released it as an open-source project.
